front: Film: the Green Destiny sword (400 years old) is stolen by Jen, apprentice of Jade Fox; Jade Fox's poison dart kills Li Mu Bai as he confesses his love to Yu Shu Lien
Film: stars Michelle Yeoh as Yu Shu Lien and Chow Yun-Fat; scored by Tan Dun; credited with reviving the wuxia genre
Film: in the final scene, Jen jumps off a bridge and glides away into a sea of fog; includes wire-work sword duel in a bamboo forest (homage to King Hu)
Film: Heath Ledger and Jake Gyllenhaal play cowboys Jack Twist and Ennis del Mar in a homosexual love affair in 1960s Wyoming; based on Annie Proulx's short story
Film: Jack says 'I wish I knew how to quit you'; Alma divorces Ennis after noticing he doesn't use his fishing pole; Ennis learns Jack was killed with a tire iron
Film: lost the 2005 Best Picture Oscar to Crash; B. Ruby Rich identified it as changing the direction of New Queer Cinema

Film: early trilogy examining Taiwanese family life — includes The Wedding Banquet (gay man in fake wedding, remade 2025 with Lily Gladstone) and Eat Drink Man Woman (widower chef cooks Sunday feasts for daughters)
Film: CGI Bengal tiger Richard Parker rendered by Rhythm & Hues (went bankrupt before winning the Oscar); Pi and Richard Parker drift for 227 days
Film: Tang Wei's debut; cinematography by Rodrigo Prieto; Chinese espionage thriller set during WWII Japanese occupation
Director: Taiwanese; second generation of the Taiwanese New Wave; known for working across varied genres from wuxia to queer cinema to Jane Austen adaptations
Director: Billy Lynn's Long Halftime Walk was shot at 120 frames per second; The Ice Storm adapted from Rick Moody's novel
